trials.supervisor_analysis: Stage 1 baseline_profile completed: score=60.20 with 5.53ms runtime (1.02x over 5.63ms baseline). Profiling revealed ConvTranspose3d at 60.5%, data transposes at 22.7%, MaxPool at 10.1%. Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: Instead of trying to fuse both maxpool layers, focus on fusing just `MaxPool3d(kernel=3)` + `sum(dim=1)` into a single Triton kernel — this eliminates the intermediate (16,64,10,10,10) tensor write/read and the channel reduction is embarrassingly parallel across spatial dims. Each Triton program handles one (batch, od, oh, ow) output element: loop over 64 channels, compute max over the 3×3×3 window from the pool1 output, and accumulate the sum. Also try `torch.compile(mode="max-autotune")` on the full model as a quick win beyond `mode="default"`.

Don't give up on the fused Triton kernel — debug it by writing a small standalone test that compares your kernel's output against `torch.sum(F.max_pool3d(pool1_output, 3), dim=1, keepdim=True)` element-by-element and prints the first few mismatches with indices. Common bugs: initializing max accumulator to `0.0` instead of `float('-inf')`, incorrect stride computation for the 5D tensor (make sure you use `pool1_output.stride()` not manual calculation), or the `tl.load` mask not properly guarding out-of-bounds pool window positions.
